cases.name: Dutch Female Passenger (Index)

flights.notes: Seat unconfirmed — boarded SHN symptomatic, deteriorated in-flight

cases.clinical_notes: Index case. Dutch female passenger (age 69) aboard MV Hondius. Before boarding in Ushuaia on April 1, she and her husband traveled in Argentina and Chile. Argentine authorities' leading hypothesis (per AP, May 7 2026): couple contracted hantavirus during a bird-watching outing in Ushuaia; investigators also tracing their route through Patagonian forested hillsides. Province of Tierra del Fuego — where ship docked — had never previously recorded a hantavirus case. Disembarked at Saint Helena on April 24 with gastrointestinal symptoms. Boarded Airlink flight 4Z 301 (SHN→JNB) on April 25 — condition deteriorated in-flight. Died April 26 at Johannesburg hospital; tested positive for Andes-strain hantavirus. WHO initiated contact tracing for all 82 passengers and 6 crew on that flight. KLM confirmed she also traveled on a KLM flight from Johannesburg. Husband (P002) died on the ship April 11. [Note: P34866 in database is likely a duplicate of this case — same person described as '69-year-old woman who disembarked at St Helena and died in South Africa'.]

cases.name: Dutch Male Passenger (Husband of Index)
cases.source_notes: Manually entered — search ProMED or WHO DON for 'Hondius hantavirus 2026'
cases.clinical_notes: Dutch male passenger (age 70), husband of index case PEDB43, sharing Cabin 406. Per WHO/USA Today May 7 official summary: developed fever, headache, and mild diarrhea on April 6; died aboard MV Hondius on April 11 — the first death in the outbreak. Argentine authorities' leading hypothesis (AP, May 7 2026): couple contracted hantavirus during a bird-watching outing in Ushuaia before boarding on April 1; investigators also tracing their route through forested hillsides of Patagonia. WHO confirmed the couple traveled Argentina and Chile prior to boarding. Tierra del Fuego province (embarkation region) had never previously recorded a hantavirus case. Body removed to St. Helena on April 24. Early reporting erroneously described him as evacuated to Punta Arenas — corrected to 'died on the ship' per WHO/AFP/Manila Times.

cases.name: British Crew Member
cases.source_notes: Manually entered — search MRCC Cape Town / SA health authorities for April 2026 evacuation records
cases.clinical_notes: Adult male passenger or crew. Per WHO/USA Today May 7 official summary (Case 3): reported fever, shortness of breath, and signs of pneumonia to the ship's doctor on April 24. Condition worsened; medically evacuated to South Africa on April 27. Currently hospitalized in intensive care unit in South Africa. Testing confirmed hantavirus infection on May 2. WHO update (May 6): patient reportedly improving.
cases.reporter: MRCC Cape Town / SA Medical Services

cases.name: Dutch Crew Member

location.venue: MV Hondius — Crew Deck 2 / Engine Room

cases.clinical_notes: Crew member sharing workspace and common ventilation zones with P003 during P003's prodromal phase. Fever, myalgia, early respiratory symptoms. Serology pending. Vessel stranded near Cape Verde — under shipboard medical monitoring awaiting evacuation. [Note: PDB8D5 is also Dutch age 41 — possible same person; retain both pending confirmation.]

cases.name: Crew Member with Mild Symptoms
cases.clinical_notes: Ill crew member with mild symptoms requiring urgent medical evacuation from the stranded cruise ship.

cases.name: Crew Member with Severe Symptoms
cases.clinical_notes: Ill crew member with severe symptoms requiring urgent medical evacuation from the stranded cruise ship.

cases.name: Dutch Doctor Patient, Serious Condition
cases.clinical_notes: A doctor on board reportedly in serious condition, to be airlifted to the Canary Islands at the Netherlands' request.

cases.name: Swiss MV Hondius Passenger
cases.clinical_notes: Man previously aboard MV Hondius cruise ship, returned from South Africa in late April with his wife, now receiving treatment at a hospital in Zurich after being notified by the ship's operator.

cases.name: Suspected Case — Fever Resolved
cases.clinical_notes: Passenger aboard MV Hondius who developed fever (key hantavirus symptom) which subsequently resolved; retained as suspected case pending PCR confirmation.

cases.name: Dutch Passenger, 41, Hondius
cases.clinical_notes: 41-year-old Dutch national evacuated from MV Hondius and flown to Amsterdam for specialized hospital treatment. [Note: P004 is also Dutch age 41, labeled crew with Apr 18 onset — possible same person; retain both pending confirmation.]

cases.name: German Passenger (Deceased, May 2)
cases.clinical_notes: Third death in the MV Hondius outbreak. Woman presented with pneumonia, first symptoms April 28. Died May 2, 2026. Nationality reported as German in early coverage. Confirmed by WHO as third fatality.

cases.name: German Passenger, 65, Hondius (suspected)
cases.clinical_notes: 65-year-old German evacuated as suspected case; asymptomatic but closely associated with a German passenger who died on May 2.

cases.name: British Man Evacuee, MV Hondius
cases.clinical_notes: British man evacuated from MV Hondius cruise ship to the Netherlands after displaying hantavirus symptoms.

cases.name: Evacuee 2, MV Hondius
cases.clinical_notes: One of three evacuees sent to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms aboard the MV Hondius.

cases.name: Cruise Ship Passenger Case 1
cases.clinical_notes: Confirmed case of Andes strain hantavirus linked to cruise ship outbreak.

cases.name: British Man Evacuee (2)
cases.clinical_notes: British man evacuated from MV Hondius to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms.

cases.name: 69-year-old woman, St Helena disembarkation
cases.source_notes: ⚠️ LIKELY DUPLICATE of PEDB43 — same person: 69-year-old Dutch female, disembarked MV Hondius at St Helena April 24, died in South Africa April 26. Retain pending deduplication review.
cases.clinical_notes: ⚠️ LIKELY DUPLICATE of PEDB43. A 69-year-old woman who disembarked from MV Hondius at St Helena April 24 and died in South Africa April 26. Per WHO and AP reporting, this matches the index case (PEDB43): Dutch female, age 69, disembarked Saint Helena April 24, died Johannesburg April 26. Both entries kept until confirmed.

cases.name: Airlink Flight Attendant (Suspected)
cases.source_notes: USA Today May 7 / dutchnews.nl / Dutch Health Ministry
cases.clinical_notes: Flight attendant potentially the first person-to-person transmission case off the ship. Was on Airlink flight 4Z 301 (SHN→JNB) with index case PEDB43 who died in Johannesburg. Showing hantavirus symptoms as of May 7. If confirmed, represents Gen 2 transmission — ship passenger to airline crew. AP reported as potentially the first off-ship infection. Dutch Health Ministry also separately noted a KLM flight attendant being tested — may be a second distinct case.
cases.reporter: Dutch Health Ministry / USA Today / dutchnews.nl

cases.name: Israeli Patient (European Strain)
cases.clinical_notes: Israeli patient confirmed with European strain hantavirus (separate from MV Hondius Andes strain). Infected in Eastern Europe several months before diagnosis in May 2026. Confirmed by antibody and PCR tests. Stable — not requiring ICU or strict isolation, under medical observation. Identity withheld by Health Ministry. Unrelated to MV Hondius cruise ship cluster.
cases.reporter: Israeli Ministry of Health / Jerusalem Post

cases.name: Virginia Resident — Under Monitoring
cases.source_notes: USA Today live updates, May 7 2026 — Virginia Dept of Health spokesperson Maria Reppas
cases.clinical_notes: One Virginia resident returned from MV Hondius, in good health, under public health monitoring as of May 7. Virginia Dept of Health says 'less than five exposed Virginians' may be identified in coming days. Virginia is the 5th US state to report returned passengers alongside California, Arizona, Texas, and Georgia. Risk to general public considered low.
cases.reporter: Virginia Department of Health / USA Today

cases.name: Texas Residents — Self-Monitoring (2)
cases.source_notes: USA Today live updates, May 7 2026 — Texas Dept of Health
cases.clinical_notes: Two Texas residents who were MV Hondius passengers returned to the state before the outbreak was identified. No symptoms reported. Left ship before contact tracing began; believed not to have had direct contact with an infected person. Agreed to self-monitor in coming days. Texas is among 5 US states (CA, AZ, TX, GA, VA) monitoring returned ship passengers.
cases.reporter: Texas Department of Health / USA Today

cases.name: Coastal Georgia Woman — MV Hondius Passenger
cases.source_notes: WTOC News, May 8 2026 — Coastal Georgia local TV report
cases.clinical_notes: Woman from coastal Georgia who was a passenger aboard MV Hondius. Returned home and is among residents being monitored by Georgia health authorities. Georgia is one of 5 US states (CA, AZ, TX, GA, VA) tracking returned ship passengers. No confirmed illness reported.
cases.reporter: WTOC / Georgia Dept of Public Health

cases.name: Rodrigo Morinigo (Argentina Domestic)

location.venue: Rural family home / Local hospital ICU

cases.source_notes: AP / Isabel Debre, May 7 2026 — San Andrés de Giles, Buenos Aires Province, Argentina
cases.clinical_notes: 14-year-old Argentine boy. Presented with fever and body aches — initially misdiagnosed as flu by local doctors in San Andrés de Giles and sent home with ibuprofen. Breathing worsened; rushed to intensive care on January 1. Died just two hours after hantavirus test came back positive. Case part of Argentina's January 2026 outbreak alert — 101 hantavirus infections recorded nationally since June 2025, roughly double the prior-year caseload. Death rate in Argentina in 2025–26 reached nearly one-third of confirmed cases, up from the 5-year average of ~15%. Andes virus (rodent-borne, not ship-related). UNRELATED to MV Hondius cruise ship cluster. Parents: Daisy Morinigo and David Delgado.

cases.name: Saint Helena Departures — Untraced (30 passengers)
cases.clinical_notes: 30 passengers from multiple countries disembarked MV Hondius at Saint Helena on April 24 — two weeks after the first passenger death on board — without contact tracing being completed (People.com, May 7 2026). Health officials across at least 5 US states (California, Arizona, Texas, Georgia, Virginia) are monitoring returned residents. WHO confirmed 5 cases + 3 suspected as of May 7. None confirmed ill in the US as of May 7. This group represents a significant untraced exposure risk across multiple countries.

edges.notes: Married couple sharing Cabin 406 for entire voyage. Onset gap of 48 h consistent with close-quarters person-to-person transmission.
British crew member provided care to index case without adequate PPE over 3-4 days. 9-day onset lag consistent with hantavirus incubation.

edges.event: Crew Common Areas / Shared Ventilation — Deck 2
edges.notes: Dutch crew member exposure likely indirect via shared spaces and ventilation with P003. Serology pending.
